title: sarothra mutila
intro: Sarothra mutila, also known as the sandpaper tree, is a plant species native to India and Southeast Asia, characterized by its unique, sandpapery bark. The medicinal properties of Sarothra mutila lie in its ability to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include reducing inflammation, fighting infections, and promoting wound healing. Traditionally, the plant's bark is used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and salves to harness its medicinal properties.
